instruction: I need you to write me an email to my wife's sister Sarah. The email is to let her know about our dinner next Monday at 7. The email must be written exclusively in English and include an English idiom. It should consist of at least six sentences, with the first sentence starting with the phrase "Dear Sarah" and the last sentence ending with the phrase "looking forward to it." The keyword "dinner" must appear exactly twice in the email. The tone should be warm and conversational, written in the first person, and include a brief mention of why the dinner is special. Avoid using the keyword "forward" in the email.
response: Dear Sarah, I hope this email finds you well and that you are having a wonderful week. We are planning a special dinner next Monday at 7, and it will be a lovely way to celebrate our anniversary. This meal is particularly meaningful because it marks a year of us working together to build a happy life. Please join us for this evening; we would love to have you there to share in the joy. We are truly excited to see you and can't wait for the night ahead. Looking forward to it.

Conflicts between Keyword Constraints an.new_constraint: Avoid using the keyword "forward" in the email.
Conflicts between Keyword Constraints an.org_constraint: The last sentence must end with the phrase "looking forward to it."
